summary: Bug 1 (pending_destock_quantity) - Backend 8/8 tests passed. All scenarios verified: (1) Stock=0, sell 30 units → over_destock=30, pending_destock_quantity=30, quantity stays 0; (2) PUT quantity=65 with pending=30 → compensation_movement (30), adjustment_movement (+35), final quantity=35, pending reset to 0; (3) No pending: PUT quantity=100 on stock=50 → simple adjustment (+50), no compensation; (4) Adjustment < pending: PUT quantity=20 with pending=30 → final=0, compensation=30, pending reset; (5) PUT without quantity change → pending unchanged, no compensation; (6) Recipe branch: ingredient in rupture sold → pending_destock_quantity incremented. Non-regression: bon_number generation and validated_only filter working.
critical_code_review_comments: IMPORTANT: Destocking only triggers when transitioning from pending to validated via PUT /api/invoices/{id}, NOT when creating directly with validation_status='validated' via POST. This is by design but should be documented.
seed_data_creation: Test data created with TEST_ITER97_ prefix, cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug 1 (pending_destock_quantity mechanism) fully tested and working. Key verified features: (1) When selling items with stock=0, the system creates a movement with over_destock field and increments pending_destock_quantity on the stock product; (2) When adjusting stock via PUT /api/stock/products/{id}, the pending backlog is automatically deducted from the new quantity (e.g., PUT 65 with pending=30 → final=35); (3) A compensation_movement is created to trace the backlog application; (4) pending_destock_quantity is reset to 0 after application; (5) Recipe ingredients also get pending_destock_quantity incremented when sold in rupture. IMPORTANT: Destocking only triggers on PUT (pending→validated transition), not on POST with validation_status='validated'.

pending_destock_scenarios: test_scenario2_adjustment_applies_pending_backlog - PASSED (PUT 65 with pending=30 → compensation=30, adjustment=+35, final=35)
test_scenario3_no_pending_simple_adjustment - PASSED (no pending, PUT 100 on stock=50 → adjustment=+50)
test_scenario6_recipe_ingredient_pending - PASSED (recipe ingredient in rupture → pending incremented)

feature_verification.compensation_movement_on_adjustment: PASSED - PUT with pending>0 creates compensation_movement
feature_verification.adjustment_movement_created: PASSED - PUT creates adjustment_movement with correct delta
feature_verification.pending_reset_after_application: PASSED - pending_destock_quantity reset to 0 after adjustment
feature_verification.no_compensation_when_no_pending: PASSED - PUT without pending does not create compensation_movement
feature_verification.no_compensation_when_no_qty_change: PASSED - PUT without quantity change does not trigger compensation
feature_verification.recipe_ingredient_pending: PASSED - Recipe ingredients get pending_destock_quantity when sold in rupture
feature_verification.bon_number_generation: PASSED - POST /api/invoices with table_number generates bon_number

mongodb_security._id_excluded: PASSED - no _id exposed in any response

bug_1_rca.issue: Stock à 0, ventes à découvert → système ne soustrait pas. Ajustement manuel doit automatiquement soustraire les ventes antérieures.
bug_1_rca.root_cause: The over_destock mechanism was implemented to track sales when stock=0. The pending_destock_quantity field accumulates the debt. When adjusting stock via PUT, the system now automatically deducts this pending amount.
bug_1_rca.fix_verification: All 6 scenarios pass. The fix correctly: (1) tracks over_destock in movements, (2) increments pending_destock_quantity, (3) applies pending on adjustment, (4) creates compensation_movement for traceability, (5) resets pending after application.
